_extract_meta.description: Session research flags B-target alignment extract for Claude AI

b_target_flags.description: EXTRACTION ANOMALY — seventh consecutive registry. H0854 et (with, 932 occ HFA preposition) incorrectly extracted. Reclassified to delete. See PH2-034-005.

EXTRACTION ANOMALY — Phase 1 source list incorrectly included 7 high-frequency grammatical function words as covenant vocabulary: H0637 (aph, also, 134 occ), H0859A (attah, you m.s., 745 occ), H1697G (davar, word, 920 occ), H1961 (hayah, to be, 3612 occ), H2063 (zot, this, 607 occ), H5973A (im, with, 979 occ), H8033G (sham, there, 838 occ). Total: 7,835 occurrences of grammatical particles misidentified as covenant terms. Diagnostic overlap analysis: 0-6% of their exported verses co-occur with berith/diatheke verses. Likely cause: Phase 1 extraction used a broad concordance sweep of covenant-context verses and captured all Strong's numbers present, including grammatical co-occurrents. Claude Code should audit the Phase 1 extraction source list for registry 34 and review whether other registries may have the same systematic error.

EXTRACTION ANOMALY — sixth consecutive registry. H4100 mah (what?, 762 occ HFA) and H0227A az (then, 122 occ HFA) incorrectly extracted. Combined 884 occurrences. Not delight vocabulary. Reclassified to delete. See PH2-034-005.

EXTRACTION ANOMALY — fifth consecutive registry (98, 34, 160, 126, 59). G1722 en (in/on/among, 10,000 occ) incorrectly extracted. Preposition with no faith-specific content. Reclassified to delete. See programme-level flag PH2-34-005.

EXTRACTION ANOMALY — fourth consecutive registry. Five HFA terms incorrectly extracted: G0846 autos (10000 occ), G1223 dia (1667), G3778 houtos (2919), H3588A ki (4499), H3820A lev (603). Combined 20,089 occurrences. Overlap with purpose vocabulary: 0-3%. Additional non-purpose bleed: G1432 freely, G2570G good, G2573 well, G2761 vainly. See programme-level flag PH2-34-005.

EXTRACTION ANOMALY — Phase 1 source list incorrectly included 5 HFA grammatical function words: H0559 (to say, 5343 occ), H0834A (which, 4974 occ), H1697G (word, 920 occ), H3588A (for, 4499 occ), G3004G (to say, 3420 occ). Combined 20,059 occurrences. Overlap with thought vocabulary: 0-4%. Same anomaly confirmed in registries 34 and 98. See programme-level flag PH2-34-005 for full investigation mandate.
EXTRACTION ANOMALY — eighth consecutive registry. H5921A al (upon, 5802 occ HFA preposition) incorrectly extracted. Reclassified to delete. See PH2-034-005.

H6293 pa.ga has three distinct senses in its 43 verses: (1) to meet/encounter (Gen 23:8; 28:11; 32:1), (2) to fall upon/attack (Num 35:19-21; Judg 8:21; 1Sa 22:17), (3) to intercede/entreat (Isa 53:12; Jer 7:16; 27:18; Ruth 1:16). Only sense (3) is prayer vocabulary. Session B analysis must distinguish intercession verses from encounter/attack verses. The 43 verses need manual sense-tagging — Claude Code cannot determine sense from the data alone.

G2171 euchē has 76 occurrences but STEP returns only 3 ESV verses (Act 18:18; 21:23; Jam 5:15). This is correct — euchē primarily means "vow" in classical and LXX usage. The 3 returned verses are the only ESV instances. The prayer sense (Jam 5:15 "the prayer of faith") is a secondary meaning. Low priority: the term is correctly extracted_thin with sufficient data for Session B analysis.
